title: v2 callback success response
description: Body the host returns with HTTP 200 after accepting a completion event delivered to the callback endpoint (see openapi/plugin-host-api.yaml). 'accepted' is the only required signal a plugin needs to stop retrying; the optional booleans give additional detail without being load-bearing for the plugin's retry decision.

accepted.description: Always true when this response is returned with HTTP 200 (the host never returns 200 with accepted: false; a rejection is instead surfaced as a 4xx/5xx status, see docs/protocol.md).

duplicate.description: true if this event_id had already been recorded (replay); the host performed no additional state change.

applied.description: true if this event caused a state transition to be recorded; false for events that were valid and authenticated but did not advance state (e.g. stale/out-of-order, see 'stale').

late.description: true if the event arrived after the host's audit TTL for its sequence position but was still within an auditable window; such events are recorded for audit purposes without regressing state.

stale.description: true if the event's sequence/state would represent a regression relative to already-recorded state for this operation_id; the event is audited but not applied.
